mnemonics: DISCONNECTED: Disconnected Is Separation Of Non-Empty Closed, Every Two Ends Disjointed
SPACES: Separated Pieces Are Clearly Everywhere Separate
keyPoints: A metric space is disconnected if it can be written as a union of two disjoint non-empty open sets
Connectedness means the space cannot be split into two separate 'islands'
The continuous image of a connected space is always connected
Intervals in R are connected, which forms the basis for the Intermediate Value Theorem
commonMistakes: Assuming all subsets of R are connected (e.g., rational numbers Q is disconnected)
Confusing path-connectedness with connectedness (path-connected implies connected, but not vice versa)
Forgetting that the closure of a connected set is always connected
